description: Two-panel bar chart comparing F1 score and average input token count for OP-RAG and long-context LLMs; caption indicates comparisons on En.QA dataset of ∞Bench.

Figure 2: Vanilla RAG versus the proposed order-preserve the RAG. As shown in the example, a long document is cropped into 13 chunks, {c_i}_{i=1}^{13}. The similarity score is appended to each chunk. We retrieve top 4 chunks with the highest similarity scores. Vanilla RAG places the chunks in a score-descending order, whereas the proposed order-preserve RAG places the chunks based on the order in the original document.
Two line charts comparing Llama3.1-8B and Llama3.1-70B on EN.QA (F1 score) and EN.MC (Accuracy) versus context length (K).
Two line charts comparing Vanilla RAG and Order-Preserve RAG as the number of retrieved chunks increases. Left: EN.QA with F1 score; right: EN.MC with Accuracy.
Comparisons among the long-context LLM without RAG, SELF-ROUTE mechanism (Li et al., 2024) and the proposed order-preserve (OP) RAG.
